heroHeadline: In Vivo CRISPR Reaches Phase 3: Intellia's Lonvo-z Achieves Historic 87% Attack Reduction in HAE
heroSubtitle: April 27, 2026: Intellia Therapeutics reports the world's first positive Phase 3 results for an in vivo CRISPR gene editing drug — lonvoguran ziclumeran eliminating attacks in 62% of HAE patients. Days later, the FDA approves Regeneron's Otarmeni as the first gene therapy for inherited hearing loss, offered free in the US. Eli Lilly commits $2.25B to AI-designed gene editing beyond CRISPR.
footerNote: Data sourced from FDA, EMA, MHRA, NIH, peer-reviewed journals (Nature, Science, NEJM, Cell), ClinicalTrials.gov, and major science outlets. Clinical data reflects published peer-reviewed results only.
